name: Melissa Aldana Quartet
description: GRAMMY nominated saxophonist and composer Melissa Aldana has released a stunning Blue Note Records ballad album, Filin, featuring Cuban songs in the gorgeous yet still-unheralded tradition of richly arranged romantic song that thrived in Cuba between the late 1940s and early 1960s. Filin the word derives from feeling creates a dialogue between traditional Cuban trova, the bolero, and jazz -- redefining Cuban musical identity. Melissa Aldana (tenor sax), Glenn Zaleski (piano), Pablo Menares (bass), and Kush Abadey (drums) capture the collective insight theyve garnered after extensive touring and travel -- arguing for their place among the most incisive working groups in jazz today.

name: The Birdland Latin Jazz Orchestra
description: The Birdland Latin Jazz Orchestra performs classic Mambos, Cha-Chas, Danzons and Salsa from legends of Latin Jazz including Machito, Tito Puente, Ray Santos, Ray Barretto, Eddie Palmieri and more. Led by saxophonist David DeJesus, who has performed with Bobby Sanabrias Big Band, Gilberto Santa Rosa, Larry Harlow, Carlos Henriquez, and the Latin Giants to name a few, the Birdland Latin Jazz Orchestra consists of some of the best musicians in New York City. Always a memorable event, David DeJesus and the Birdland Latin Jazz Orchestra are a must see in NYC!
